problem_statement: Knowledge base search is using prefix matching, so articles are only returned when the query matches the start of the topic/title/content field. Queries that appear later in the text are not found.
goal: Update knowledge base search so it performs a contains/substring match across topic, title, and content, returning any article where the query appears anywhere within those fields.
acceptance_criteria: Searching for a term that appears in the middle of an article's topic/title/content returns that article.
Existing prefix matches continue to work.
Search behavior is consistent across all searchable knowledge article fields.
constraints: Likely limited to backend search logic; avoid unrelated schema/API changes unless required.
Preserve existing result shape and ordering unless the search implementation requires otherwise.
unknowns: Whether the search is implemented in Python filtering or delegated to the database query layer.
Whether any normalization (case folding, trimming, tokenization) is already applied in the current search path.
repo_summary: Backend service for knowledge base search appears to live under backend/app/services, with route and model definitions in adjacent files. The issue explicitly points to backend/app/services/knowledge_service.py search as the affected location.

targeted_symbols.reason: Issue explicitly identifies the search method as the affected area; likely where prefix matching is implemented and should be changed to substring matching.

localization_rationale: The bug description clearly isolates search semantics rather than data shape, API routing, or model schema.
The affected file is explicitly named as backend/app/services/knowledge_service.py (search), making it the smallest likely implementation area.
Only the matching predicate needs to change from starts-with to contains across the knowledge article fields.
